page: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-48 is an x86_64 architecture general-purpose server powered by an Intel processor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The system is equipped with 192 GB of RAM, providing a balanced memory-to-vCPU ratio of 4.0 GB per core. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. It lacks local storage and bundled GPUs, and does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark evaluations show strong performance in memory bandwidth, with read-only small block speeds reaching 3417.19 GB/sec and large block write speeds at 114.19 GB/sec. CPU-intensive tasks perform well, as evidenced by a PassMark CPU score of 58480.28 and strong results in extended instructions, encryption, and physics tests. The server demonstrates average performance in single-core stress-ng tests and software compilation, but excels in multi-core Geekbench tasks, ray tracing, and static web serving throughput, which reaches 67.45 GB/sec.

The processor supports advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512), which accelerate cryptographic and integer operations. These hardware features translate to strong performance in small-model LLM prompt processing (2557.3 tokens/sec) and database operations (23304.66 on PassMark). This configuration is highly suitable for enterprise database operations, multi-threaded web serving, medium-scale machine learning inference, and general-purpose enterprise workloads requiring balanced compute and memory without local storage requirements.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-48 is a general-purpose server featuring 48 dedicated vCPUs and 192 GB of RAM on an x86_64 Intel architecture. It provides a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core, backed by a 260 MB L3 cache, but includes no local storage or GPUs. The server delivers strong multi-core performance, highlighted by high memory bandwidth, a PassMark CPU score of 58480.28, and exceptional static web serving throughput of 67.45 GB/sec. Supported by Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets, it excels at cryptographic tasks, database operations, and small-to-medium LLM inference. This server is highly cost-efficient for resource-dense general-purpose workloads, enterprise applications, and multi-threaded web serving.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-48 is an x86_64 general-purpose server with 48 dedicated Intel vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and no local storage, offering strong multi-core performance and high memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-48 is a general-purpose server with 48 Intel vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and strong multi-core and database performance.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose server featuring 48 dedicated vCPUs, high memory bandwidth, and advanced hardware acceleration for enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated Intel vCPUs and 192 GB RAM on an x86_64 architecture.
Strong memory bandwidth with read-only speeds up to 3417.19 GB/sec.
Advanced instruction support including Intel AMX and AVX-512.
Excellent fit for database operations, static web serving, and multi-threaded applications.
No local storage or bundled GPUs, optimizing cost for pure compute and memory needs.
